name: Customer Journey Map
summary: A visual representation of every touchpoint a customer has with your brand, from first awareness to post-purchase loyalty.
whenToUseIt: Use journey mapping when you want to improve customer experience, identify drop-off points, align your team on the customer perspective, or plan content/campaigns for each stage.
steps: Define your persona: Choose a specific customer segment to map
List the stages: Awareness → Consideration → Decision → Purchase → Post-purchase → Advocacy
Map touchpoints: At each stage, list every interaction (ads, website, emails, reviews, support, etc.)
Capture emotions: How does the customer feel at each stage? Frustrated? Excited? Confused?
Identify pain points: Where do they struggle or drop off?
Find opportunities: Where can you improve the experience or add value?
example: Journey map for an online furniture store: Awareness (sees Instagram ad) → Consideration (visits website, reads reviews, visits showroom) → Decision (adds to cart, compares delivery options — pain point: high delivery cost nearly causes abandonment) → Purchase (uses Afterpay) → Post-purchase (tracking updates reduce anxiety) → Advocacy (shares photo on Instagram after delivery). The insight: offering free delivery over $500 would reduce the biggest drop-off point.
